hypothesis: Mutating position 5 to aromatic residues (W/F/Y) will enhance SSTR2-specific interactions with ECL2 and TM5, while mutating position 11 to hydrophobic residues (L/I/V) will improve steric complementarity with SSTR2-unique TM6 pocket, increasing selectivity margin over off-targets.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis remains valid, but the focus must shift to fine-tuning mutations at non-pharmacophore positions to enhance selectivity over SSTR2. The next iteration should prioritize disrupting off-target interactions while preserving the pharmacophore.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are known to contribute to off-target interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5. Focusing mutations here can disrupt conserved-pocket contacts without affecting the pharmacophore (FWKT7-10 and Cys3/Cys14).
These mutations are designed to disrupt conserved interactions with SSTR3/SSTR5 while maintaining flexibility and avoiding steric clashes.
